function solve(m, n, waitCost) {

   // Initialize the distance array

   const maxTime = (m + n) * 2;

   const INF = Infinity;

   const dist = new Array(m).fill().map(() => new Array(n).fill().map(() => new Array(maxTime + 1).fill(INF)));

   // The initial state is (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1

   dist[0][0][1] = 1;

   // Priority queue: [cost, i, j, time]

   const pq = [[1, 0, 0, 1]];

   // Directions for moving: right and down

   const directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]];

   // Dijkstra's algorithm

   while (pq.length > 0) {

      const [cost, i, j, t] = pq.shift(); // Using shift for simplicity, but in practice, a heap is better

      // Check if current cell is destination

      if (i === m - 1 && j === n - 1) {

         return cost;

      }

      // If current cost is higher than the recorded distance, skip

      if (cost > dist[i][j][t]) {

         continue;

      }

      // Determine the next action based on t's parity

      if (t % 2 === 1) { // odd: move

         // Try to move right or down

         for (const [di, dj] of directions) {

             const ni = i + di;

             const nj = j + dj;

             if (ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n) {

                 const new_time = t + 1;

                 const new_cost = cost + (ni + 1) * (nj + 1);

                 if (new_cost < dist[ni][nj][new_time]) {

                     dist[ni][nj][new_time] = new_cost;

                     pq.push([new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]);

                 }

             }

         }

      } else { // even: wait

         const new_time = t + 1;

         const new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j];

         if (new_cost < dist[i][j][new_time]) {

             dist[i][j][new_time] = new_cost;

             pq.push([new_cost, i, j, new_time]);

         }

      }

   }

   // If not found (should not happen as per problem statement)

   return -1;

}

function solve(m, n, waitCost) {

   // Initialize the distance array

   const maxTime = (m + n) * 2;

   const INF = Infinity;

   const dist = new Array(m).fill().map(() => new Array(n).fill().map(() => new Array(maxTime + 1).fill(INF)));

   // The initial state is (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1

   dist[0][0][1] = 1;

   // Priority queue: [cost, i, j, time]

   const pq = [[1, 0, 0, 1]];

   // Directions for moving: right and down

   const directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]];

   // Dijkstra's algorithm

   while (pq.length > 0) {

      const [cost, i, j, t] = pq.shift(); // Using shift for simplicity, but in practice, a heap is better

      // Check if current cell is destination

      if (i === m - 1 && j === n - 1) {

         return cost;

      }

      // If current cost is higher than the recorded distance, skip

      if (cost > dist[i][j][t]) {

         continue;

      }

      // Determine the next action based on t's parity

      if (t % 2 === 1) { // odd: move

         // Try to move right or down

         for (const [di, dj] of directions) {

             const ni = i + di;

             const nj = j + dj;

             if (ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n) {

                 const new_time = t + 1;

                 const new_cost = cost + (ni + 1) * (nj + 1);

                 if (new_cost < dist[ni][nj][new_time]) {

                     dist[ni][nj][new_time] = new_cost;

                     pq.push([new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]);

                 }

             }

         }

      } else { // even: wait

         const new_time = t + 1;

         const new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j];

         if (new_cost < dist[i][j][new_time]) {

             dist[i][j][new_time] = new_cost;

             pq.push([new_cost, i, j, new_time]);

         }

      }

   }

   // If not found (should not happen as per problem statement)

   return -1;

}
